title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
principle: Don't fetch a URL the user controls. If an outbound request must use user input, allowlist the exact hosts (or schemes and ports) you permit, resolve the host and reject private, loopback, and link-local addresses (including the cloud metadata IP 169.254.169.254), and disable redirect following so an allowed host can't bounce you inward.

snippets.library: requests with an explicit host allowlist and redirects disabled
snippets.secure:
host = urlparse(user_url).hostname
if host not in ALLOWED_HOSTS:
    abort(400)
resp = requests.get(user_url, allow_redirects=False, timeout=5)
snippets.note: Allowlist by host, not by substring ("evil.com/trusted.com" defeats substring checks). For defense in depth, resolve the host and reject private ranges with ipaddress.ip_address(...).is_private.

snippets.library: an allowlist plus a private-IP check before fetch
snippets.note: redirect: 'error' stops an allowed host from redirecting to an internal one. Resolve and block private/loopback ranges (e.g. with the 'ip' package) as a second layer.

snippets.library: a validated URL against an allowlist, redirects off
snippets.note: Also resolve InetAddress.getByName(host) and reject isSiteLocalAddress / isLoopbackAddress / isLinkLocalAddress.

snippets.library: net/http with a redirect guard and a private-IP dial check
snippets.note: For strong protection set a custom Transport.DialContext that resolves the address and rejects private/loopback/link-local IPs, so DNS rebinding can't slip through.
